generatedSentence: Norman, a handsome but unliked gladiator, entered the witchy jungle, making a hundred buccaneers give salutations from their junker jukebox, fueled by varietal kerosene; their picky governor peered through a window at the divisible tracks, gaining insight into humankind's future grinder. With sufficient kerosene, the lander’s director, a cognizant huntress, made voyages, her duple earplug buzzing as hacky electric impulses gave her a crucial diamond insight into the inscrutable dorkiest boyband; she chuckled, knowing their naive jealousies and hokey goodwill could be jeopardized imminently by dastardly forces. The newly injected blood, charged with noxious particles, began to debilitate the utilitarian muscles of the islanders, whose contribution was meant to fortify their backbone; contact was lost as they breakdanced, bonkers from the rude, clashing energy, making the department’s elimination of the pastel powwow a heliocentric history. Through the distance, a vigilant vocalist, our savior, observed monkeys rolling rock-hard cucumbers, their salmon kidneys awfully inflamed by magnets; she flagged her jeep, her navy tongs ready for a final exchange in the gunky waters, silently whispering goodbye to the disappointed, recommending a careful neutralizing of the duple prickle before it finished.

With sufficient kerosene, the lander’s director, a cognizant huntress, made voyages, her duple earplug buzzing as hacky electric impulses gave her a crucial diamond insight into the inscrutable dorkiest boyband; she chuckled, knowing their naive jealousies and hokey goodwill could be jeopardized imminently by dastardly forces.

The newly injected blood, charged with noxious particles, began to debilitate the utilitarian muscles of the islanders, whose contribution was meant to fortify their backbone; contact was lost as they breakdanced, bonkers from the rude, clashing energy, making the department’s elimination of the pastel powwow a heliocentric history.

Through the distance, a vigilant vocalist, our savior, observed monkeys rolling rock-hard cucumbers, their salmon kidneys awfully inflamed by magnets; she flagged her jeep, her navy tongs ready for a final exchange in the gunky waters, silently whispering goodbye to the disappointed, recommending a careful neutralizing of the duple prickle before it finished.
rawPanelSentences: Norman, a handsome but unliked gladiator, entered the witchy jungle, making a hundred buccaneers give salutations from their junker jukebox, fueled by varietal kerosene; their picky governor peered through a window at the divisible tracks, gaining insight into humankind's future grinder.

With sufficient kerosene, the lander’s director, a cognizant huntress, made voyages, her duple earplug buzzing as hacky electric impulses gave her a crucial diamond insight into the inscrutable dorkiest boyband; she chuckled, knowing their naive jealousies and hokey goodwill could be jeopardized imminently by dastardly forces.

The newly injected blood, charged with noxious particles, began to debilitate the utilitarian muscles of the islanders, whose contribution was meant to fortify their backbone; contact was lost as they breakdanced, bonkers from the rude, clashing energy, making the department’s elimination of the pastel powwow a heliocentric history.

Through the distance, a vigilant vocalist, our savior, observed monkeys rolling rock-hard cucumbers, their salmon kidneys awfully inflamed by magnets; she flagged her jeep, her navy tongs ready for a final exchange in the gunky waters, silently whispering goodbye to the disappointed, recommending a careful neutralizing of the duple prickle before it finished.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined female gladiator, wearing a custom-fitted chrome-silver chest plate and a flowing dark crimson cape. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Female gladiator entering a dense, glowing alien jungle, ancient tech vines around her, low angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Female gladiator, now a huntress, piloting a sleek, futuristic lander, viewing a holographic map of a distant pastel planet, eye-level shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Female gladiator, now a savior, observing a chaotic scene of silhouetted figures clashing with glowing particles on a moonlit, alien beach, wide shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Female gladiator, standing victorious beside her advanced jeep near a tranquil, bioluminescent alien ocean, watching a final, peaceful sunset, medium shot.

definitions.definition: Normie
A wooden bar, or iron pin.
A country's entire sea force, including ships and personnel.
A governmental department in charge of a country's sea force.
A dark blue colour, usually called navy blue.

Belonging to the navy; typical of the navy.
A picture.
Fussy; particular; demanding to have things just right.

definitions.definition: A mark left by something that has passed along.

A road or other similar beaten path.

definitions.definition: Physical course; way.

definitions.definition: A path or course laid out for a race, for exercise, etc.

definitions.definition: The direction and progress of someone or something; path.

definitions.definition: A tract or area, such as of land.
The street, as a prostitute's place of work.
Awareness of something, especially when arising from close monitoring.
The distance between two opposite wheels on a same axletree.
Short for caterpillar track.
The pitch.
Sound stored on a record.
The physical track on a record.

To continue over time.

definitions.definition: Straw or hay cut up fine for the food of cattle.
Any excess or unwanted material, resource, or person; anything worthless.
Light jesting talk; banter; raillery.
Loose material, e.g. small strips of aluminum foil dropped from aircraft, intended to interfere with radar detection.
An opening, usually covered by one or more panes of clear glass, to allow light and air from outside to enter a building or vehicle.

definitions.definition: A restricted range.

A rectangular area on a computer terminal or screen containing some kind of user interface, displaying the output of and allowing input for one of a number of simultaneously running computer processes.

The time between first infection and detectability.
To furnish with windows.
To place at or in a window.
The chief executive officer of a first-level division of a country.
A device which regulates or controls some action of a machine through automatic feedback.

A member of a decision-making for an organization or entity (including some public agencies) similar to or equivalent to a board of directors (used especially for banks); a member of the board of governors.
Father.
Boss, employer, similar to gaffer.
Term of address to a man; guv'nor.
(grammar) A constituent of a phrase that governs another.

A pilot; a steersman.

To compete in a public contest.
To debate or argue.
To act aggressively toward others.
Any substance that can be divided.
Capable of being divided or split.

definitions.definition: To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.

Often also doubles as a fundraiser, or can be held in conjunction with a non-indigneous fair or exhibition in order to attract a large crowd, as at the Calgary Stampede and K-Days.

To push or pump (something, especially fluids) into a cavity or passage.

definitions.definition: To introduce (something) suddenly or violently.
definitions.example: Punk injected a much-needed sense of urgency into the British music scene.

definitions.definition: To administer an injection to (someone or something), especially of medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: Now lie back while we inject you with the anesthetic.

definitions.definition: To take or be administered something by means of injection, especially medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: It's been a week since I stopped injecting, and I'm still in withdrawal.

definitions.definition: To introduce (code) into an existing program or its memory space, often without tight integration and sometimes through a security vulnerability.
To cast or throw; used with on.
The series of vertebrae, separated by disks, that encloses and protects the spinal cord, and runs down the middle of the back in vertebrate animals.
Any fundamental support, structure, or infrastructure.
definitions.example: Before automobiles, railroads were a backbone of commerce.

definitions.definition: Courage, fortitude, or strength.
definitions.example: He would make a good manager, if he had a little more backbone.

definitions.definition: One who bonks.
Mad; crazy.
The act of eliminating, expelling or throwing off.
The act of excluding a losing contestant from a match, tournament, or other competition.
The act of voting off or throwing off a contestant in a reality television competition.
The act of discharging or excreting waste products or foreign substances through the various emunctories.

The act of causing a quantity to disappear from an equation; especially, in the operation of deducing from several equations containing several unknown quantities a less number of equations containing a less number of unknown quantities.
The act of obtaining by separation, or as the result of eliminating; deduction.
The act of recording amounts in a consolidation statement to remove the effects of inter-company transactions.
The aggregate of past events.
definitions.example: History repeats itself if we don’t learn from its mistakes.

definitions.definition: The branch of knowledge that studies the past; the assessment of notable events.
definitions.example: He teaches history at the university.   History will not look kindly on these tyrants.   He dreams of an invention that will make history.

definitions.definition: A set of events involving an entity.
definitions.example: What is your medical history?   The family's history includes events best forgotten.

definitions.definition: A record or narrative description of past events.
definitions.example: I really enjoyed Shakespeare's tragedies more than his histories.

definitions.definition: A list of past and continuing medical conditions of an individual or family.
definitions.example: A personal medical history is required for the insurance policy.   He has a history of cancer in his family.

definitions.definition: A record of previous user events, especially of visited web pages in a browser.
definitions.example: I visited a great site yesterday but forgot the URL. Luckily, I didn't clear my history.

definitions.definition: Something that no longer exists or is no longer relevant.
definitions.example: I told him that if he doesn't get his act together, he's history.

definitions.definition: Shared experience or interaction.
definitions.example: He has had a lot of history with the police.

definitions.definition: To narrate or record.
Having the sun at the center/centre; usually in reference to a solar system or orbit.
A piece of material that attracts some metals by magnetism.
(preceded by a noun) A person or thing that attracts what is denoted by the preceding noun.
definitions.example: He always had a girl on his arm – he's a bit of a babe magnet.

definitions.definition: Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
definitions.example: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

definitions.definition: Any nonhuman simian primate, including apes.
definitions.example: Chimpanzees are known to form bands to hunt and kill other monkeys.

definitions.definition: A mischievous child.
definitions.example: She's a cheeky monkey.

definitions.definition: A dance move popular in the 1960s.
Five hundred pounds sterling; five hundred dollars.
A person or the role of the person on the sidecar platform of a motorcycle involved in sidecar racing.
A person with minimal intelligence and/or an unattractive appearance
A face card.
A menial employee who does a repetitive job, as in code monkey, grease monkey, phone monkey, powder monkey.

The weight or hammer of a pile driver; a heavy mass of iron, which, being raised high, falls on the head of the pile, and drives it into the earth; the falling weight of a drop hammer used in forging.
A small trading vessel of the sixteenth century.
A drug habit; an addiction; a compulsion.
A fluid consisting of hydrochloric acid and zinc, used in the process of soldering.
A person's temper, said to be "up" when they are angry.
A black person.
To meddle; to mess (with).
definitions.example: Please don't monkey with the controls if you don't know what you're doing.

definitions.definition: To mimic; to ape.
A naive person; a greenhorn.
Lacking worldly experience, wisdom, or judgement; unsophisticated.
Not having been exposed to something.
(of art) Produced in a simple, childlike style, deliberately rejecting sophisticated techniques.
Intuitive; designed to follow the way ordinary people approach a problem.
Harmful; injurious.
Very recently; in the immediate past.

definitions.definition: The amount of money levied for a service.

definitions.definition: A ground attack against a prepared enemy.
definitions.example: Pickett did not die leading his famous charge.

definitions.definition: A forceful forward movement.
An accusation.
definitions.example: That's a slanderous charge of abuse of trust.

definitions.definition: An electric charge.
The scope of someone's responsibility.

definitions.definition: Someone or something entrusted to one's care, such as a child to a babysitter or a student to a teacher.
definitions.example: The child was a charge of the nanny.

definitions.definition: A load or burden; cargo.

definitions.definition: An instruction.

definitions.definition: An offensive foul in which the player with the ball moves into a stationary defender.
A measured amount of powder and/or shot in a firearm cartridge.
An image displayed on an escutcheon.
A position (of a weapon) fitted for attack.

definitions.definition: A sort of plaster or ointment.
Weight; import; value.
A measure of thirty-six pigs of lead, each pig weighing about seventy pounds; a charre.
An address given at a church service concluding a visitation.
To assign a duty or responsibility to
To assign (a debit) to an account
definitions.example: Let's charge this to marketing.

definitions.definition: To pay on account, as by using a credit card
definitions.example: Can I charge my purchase to my credit card?

definitions.definition: To require payment (of) (a price or fee, for goods, services, etc.)
definitions.example: I won't charge you for the wheat

definitions.definition: (possibly archaic) to sell at a given price.
definitions.example: to charge coal at $5 per unit

definitions.definition: To formally accuse (a person) of a crime.
definitions.example: I'm charging you with assault and battery.

definitions.definition: To impute or ascribe
To call to account; to challenge
To place a burden or load on or in
To load equipment with material required for its use, as a firearm with powder, a fire hose with water, a chemical reactor with raw materials
definitions.example: Charge your weapons; we're moving up.

definitions.definition: To move forward quickly and forcefully, particularly in combat and/or on horseback
(of a hunting dog) to lie on the belly and be still (A command given by a hunter to a dog)
A vine in the gourd family, Cucumis sativus.
The edible fruit of this plant, having a green rind and crisp white flesh.
Something given or offered that adds to a larger whole.
An amount of money given toward something.
The act of contributing.
The taking part, often with the idea that it has led to (scientific etc.) progress.
A small, blocky, military-style vehicle with four-wheel drive, suited to rough terrain.
A light utility truck from WWII used by the U.S. Army, and subsequently turned into the trademark Jeep when civilianized by originating manufacturer Willys-Overland.
(genericized trademark) A sport utility vehicle or similar four-wheel-drive vehicle.
To travel by jeep.
Pertaining to utility or general purpose.
To furnish or deck out with flags.
To mark with a flag, especially to indicate the importance of something.
(often with down) To signal to, especially to stop a passing vehicle etc.

definitions.definition: To convey (a message) by means of flag signals.

definitions.definition: (often with up) To note, mark or point out for attention.

definitions.definition: To signal (an event).

definitions.definition: To set a program variable to true.

definitions.definition: To decoy (game) by waving a flag, handkerchief, etc. to arouse the animal's curiosity.
To penalize for an infraction.

definitions.definition: To defeat (an opponent) on time, especially in a blitz game.
To weaken, become feeble.

definitions.definition: To hang loose without stiffness; to bend down, as flexible bodies; to be loose, yielding, limp.
To let droop; to suffer to fall, or let fall, into feebleness.

definitions.definition: To enervate; to exhaust the vigour or elasticity of.

definitions.definition: Marked with a flag
Registered with a particular country (and thus flying its flag)
A formation of minerals, specifically:

definitions.definition: Something that is strong, stable, and dependable; a person who provides security or support to another.
A lump or cube of ice.

definitions.definition: A type of confectionery made from sugar in the shape of a stick, traditionally having some text running through its length.
definitions.example: While we're in Brighton, let's get a stick of rock!

definitions.definition: A crystallized lump of crack cocaine.
An unintelligent person, especially one who repeats mistakes.
An Afrikaner.
An extremely conservative player who is willing to play only the very strongest hands.
Any of several fish:
A basketball.
definitions.example: Yo homie, pass the rock!

definitions.definition: (rock paper scissors) A closed hand (a handshape resembling a rock), that beats scissors and loses to paper. It beats lizard and loses to Spock in rock-paper-scissors-lizard-Spock.
A cricket ball, especially a new one that has not been softened by use
A crystal used to control the radio frequency.
An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.
This organ (of an animal) cooked as food.
Constitution, temperament, nature, type, character, disposition. (usually used of people)
A waiter.

An instrument or tool used for picking things up without touching them with the hands or fingers, consisting of two slats or grips hinged at the end or in the middle, and sometimes including a spring to open the grips.

(by extension) A large scissors-like two-piece center-hinged forged-iron implement with oval-loop handles and with pointed tips turned inward (in the same plane as and perpendicular to the handles) to facilitate lifting and carrying a block of ice. Often called ice tongs.

A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.
definitions.example: By the action of electricity, the water was resolved into its two parts, oxygen and hydrogen.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned liquid, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
definitions.example: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

definitions.definition: Water in a body; an area of open water.
definitions.example: The boat was found within the territorial waters.

definitions.definition: A body of water, almost always a river.
A combination of water and other substance(s).
(in the plural or in the singular) A state of affairs; conditions; usually with an adjective indicating an adverse condition.

definitions.definition: A person's intuition.
definitions.example: I know he'll succeed. I feel it in my waters.

definitions.definition: Excess valuation of securities.
The limpidity and lustre of a precious stone, especially a diamond.
definitions.example: a diamond of the first water is perfectly pure and transparent

definitions.definition: A wavy, lustrous pattern or decoration such as is imparted to linen, silk, metals, etc.
To pour water into the soil surrounding (plants).
To wet or supply with water; to moisten; to overflow with water; to irrigate.
To provide (animals) with water for drinking.

definitions.definition: To urinate onto.
To dilute.

definitions.definition: To overvalue (securities), especially through deceptive accounting.
To fill with or secrete water.

Polite and well-mannered.
Stylish or elegant.
Aristocratic
Greasy, messy or dirty.
Like a dork.

To fail to meet (an expectation); to fail to fulfil (a hope).
To show (an opinion, belief, etc.) to be mistaken.
To prevent (something planned or attempted).
Defeated of expectations or hope; experiencing disappointment; let down.

definitions.definition: Expressing or indicating disappointment.

definitions.definition: A member of the Los Angeles gang The Bloods.

A vital liquid flowing in the bodies of many types of animals that usually conveys nutrients and oxygen. In vertebrates, it is colored red by hemoglobin, is conveyed by arteries and veins, is pumped by the heart and is usually generated in bone marrow.
definitions.example: The cultists gathered around a chalice of blood.

definitions.definition: A family relationship due to birth, such as that between siblings; contrasted with relationships due to marriage or adoption (see blood relative, blood relation, by blood).
One of the four humours in the human body.
A blood test or blood sample.
The sap or juice which flows in or from plants.
The juice of anything, especially if red.
Temper of mind; disposition; mood
A lively, showy man; a rake; a dandy.
A blood horse, one of good pedigree.
Bloodshed.
definitions.example: They came looking for blood.

definitions.definition: To cause something to be covered with blood; to bloody.
To let blood (from); to bleed.
To initiate into warfare or a blood sport, traditionally by smearing with the blood of the first kill witnessed.
An act of exchanging or trading.
definitions.example: All in all, it was an even exchange.

definitions.definition: A place for conducting trading.
definitions.example: The stock exchange is open for trading.

definitions.definition: A telephone exchange.
The fourth through sixth digits of a ten-digit phone number (the first three before the introduction of area codes).
definitions.example: NPA-NXX-1234 is standard format, where NPA is the area code and NXX is the exchange.

definitions.definition: A conversation.
definitions.example: After an exchange with the manager, we were no wiser.

definitions.definition: The loss of one piece and associated capture of another
The thing given or received in return; especially, a publication exchanged for another.
The transfer of substances or elements like gas, amino-acids, ions etc. sometimes through a surface like a membrane.
The difference between the values of money in different places.
One of several species of fish, typically of the Salmoninae subfamily, brownish above with silvery sides and delicate pinkish-orange flesh; they ascend rivers to spawn.
(plural salmons) A pale pinkish-orange colour, the colour of cooked salmon.
The upper bricks in a kiln which receive the least heat.
Snout (tobacco; from salmon and trout)
To ride a bicycle the wrong way down a one-way street.
Having a pale pinkish-orange colour.
Badly, terribly.

definitions.definition: Very; exceedingly; extremely; excessively.
In a manner inspiring awe.
Reverently.
Fearfully.
A contractile form of tissue which animals use to effect movement.

definitions.definition: An organ composed of muscle tissue.
(usually in the plural) A well-developed physique, in which the muscles are enlarged from exercise.
Strength, force.
Hired strongmen or bodyguards.
To use force to make progress, especially physical force.

Of or relating to utility
Pertaining to utilitarianism
Practical and functional, present for use, not just for show.
To make even, inactive or ineffective.

definitions.definition: To make (an acidic or alkaline substance) chemically neutral.
To kill.
Watchful, especially for danger or disorder; alert; wary

definitions.definition: To cause to make a clashing sound.
To come into violent conflict.

definitions.definition: To argue angrily.

definitions.definition: To chatter or gossip.
A clash; the sound or action of clashing; violent collision.
Conflicting, contrasting, or contrary; inconsonant, incompatible, or irreconcilable
Mismatched, inharmonious, discordant or dissonant, not fitting well together (especially in regards to aesthetics, colors, clothing, or sounds)
Adverse or antagonistic; disagreeing or opposing
The act of touching physically; being in close association.
The establishment of communication (with).

Any of various physical objects making up the constituent parts of an atom; an elementary particle or subatomic particle.
A word that has a particular grammatical function but does not obviously belong to any particular part of speech, such as the word to in English infinitives or O as a vocative particle.

In the Roman Catholic church, a crumb of consecrated bread; also the smaller breads used in the communion of the laity.

definitions.definition: A part, portion, or subdivision.

definitions.definition: A subdivision of an organization.

A territorial division; a district; especially, in France, one of the districts into which the country is divided for governmental purposes, similar to a county in the UK and in the USA. France is composed of 101 départements organized in 18 régions, each department is divided into arrondissements, in turn divided into cantons.
A military subdivision of a country
definitions.example: the Department of the Potomac

definitions.definition: Act of departing; departure.
The amount of space between two points, usually geographical points, usually (but not necessarily) measured along a straight line.

definitions.definition: Length or interval of time.
The difference; the subjective measure between two quantities.

definitions.definition: Remoteness of place; a remote place.

definitions.definition: A withholding of intimacy; alienation; variance.

To dance in this style.
